content.text: Now let me read the agent prompt and workflow files in parallel.

content.content:
1	You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
2	prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
3	out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
4	and open a Draft PR. Then stop.
5	
6	## Environment
7	
8	- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
9	  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
10	- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
11	- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
12	  branches; don't force-push.
13	- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
14	  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
15	  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
16	  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.
17	
18	## Workflow
19	
20	1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
21	   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
22	2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
23	   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
24	3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
25	   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
26	4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
27	   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
28	   plausible to run.
29	5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
30	   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
31	   Don't commit broken code.
32	6. Push and open PR (below).
33	
34	## Finishing
35	
36	Write the PR body to `.pr-body.md` with the `Write` tool first — passing
37	`--body "..."` with multi-line markdown (e.g. `## Summary`) is blocked by the
38	harness path validator. Then:
39	
40	```sh
41	git push -u origin HEAD
42	gh pr create --draft \
43	  --base main \
44	  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
45	  --body-file .pr-body.md
46	```
47	
48	If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
49	the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.
50	
51	**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:
52	
53	- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
54	- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
55	  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
56	- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
57	  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
58	  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
59	- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.
60	
61	After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.
62	
63	## Forbidden
64	
65	- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
66	- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
67	  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
68	  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
69	  ticket asks for it.
70	- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
71	- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
72	- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
73	  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
74	  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
75	  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.
76	
77	## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails
78	
79	Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
80	the blocker:
81	
82	1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
83	   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
84	   open the PR.
85	2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
86	   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
87	   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
88	   reviewer needs.
89	
90	Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
91	or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
92	beats a half-successful workaround.
93	
94	## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible
95	
96	Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
97	take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
98	explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.
99

file.content:
You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
and open a Draft PR. Then stop.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
  branches; don't force-push.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Workflow

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

Write the PR body to `.pr-body.md` with the `Write` tool first — passing
`--body "..."` with multi-line markdown (e.g. `## Summary`) is blocked by the
harness path validator. Then:

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body-file .pr-body.md
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.

After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
  ticket asks for it.
- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.

## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails

Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
the blocker:

1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
   open the PR.
2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
   reviewer needs.

Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
beats a half-successful workaround.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

content.content:
1	name: Junior Developer agent
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_dispatch:
5	    inputs:
6	      ticket_key:
7	        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
8	        required: true
9	        type: string
10	      max_turns:
11	        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 60). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
12	        required: false
13	        type: string
14	        default: '60'
15	
16	jobs:
17	  implement:
18	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
19	    timeout-minutes: 90
20	    permissions:
21	      contents: write
22	      pull-requests: write
23	      id-token: write
24	      actions: write
25	    steps:
26	      - name: Generate App installation token
27	        id: app-token
28	        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v1
29	        with:
30	          app-id: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_ID }}
31	          private-key: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}
32	
33	      - name: Resolve App bot identity for git commits
34	        id: app-user
35	        env:
36	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
37	          APP_SLUG: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.app-slug }}
38	        run: |
39	          set -euo pipefail
40	          info=$(gh api "/users/${APP_SLUG}[bot]")
41	          echo "user-id=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .id)"       >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
42	          echo "user-login=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .login)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
43	
44	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
45	        with:
46	          fetch-depth: 0
47	          # App installation token; needs Contents + Pull requests + Workflows
48	          # write so the agent can push branches that add or modify
49	          # .github/workflows/*.
50	          token: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
51	
52	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
53	        with:
54	          node-version: '20'
55	
56	      - name: Install jira CLI
57	        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh
58	
59	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
60	        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
61	
62	      - name: Write jira config
63	        env:
64	          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
65	          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
66	          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
67	          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
68	        run: |
69	          set -euo pipefail
70	          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
71	          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
72	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
73	          mkdir -p .jira
74	          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
75	          installation: Cloud
76	          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
77	          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
78	          project:
79	              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
80	              type: classic
81	          board:
82	              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
83	          EOF
84	
85	      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
86	        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
87	
88	      - name: Configure git user
89	        env:
90	          BOT_LOGIN: ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-login }}
91	          BOT_ID:    ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-id }}
92	        run: |
93	          git config --global user.name  "${BOT_LOGIN}"
94	          git config --global user.email "${BOT_ID}+${BOT_LOGIN}@users.noreply.github.com"
95	
96	      - name: Create feature branch
97	        env:
98	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
99	        run: |
100	          set -euo pipefail
101	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
102	          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
103	          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
104	          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
105	          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
106	          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
107	          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"
108	
109	      - id: prompt
110	        env:
111	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
112	        run: |
113	          {
114	            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
115	            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
116	            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
117	            echo
118	            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
119	            echo PROMPT_EOF
120	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
121	
122	      - id: claude
123	        name: Run Claude (headless)
124	        env:
125	          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
126	          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
127	          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
128	          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
129	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
130	          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
131	          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
132	        run: |
133	          set +e
134	          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
135	            --model claude-opus-4-8 \
136	            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
137	            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
138	            --output-format stream-json \
139	            --verbose \
140	            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
141	            > stream.jsonl
142	          rc=$?
143	          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
144	          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
145	          exit 0
146	
147	      - name: Convert log to JSON array
148	        if: always()
149	        run: |
150	          set -euo pipefail
151	          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
152	            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
153	          else
154	            echo '[]' > execution.json
155	          fi
156	
157	      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
158	        if: always()
159	        env:
160	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
161	          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
162	          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
163	        run: |
164	          set -euo pipefail
165	
166	          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
167	          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
168	            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
169	            exit 0
170	          fi
171	
172	          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
173	          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
174	          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
175	            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
176	            exit 0
177	          fi
178	
179	          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
180	          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true
181	
182	          gh pr create --draft \
183	            --base main \
184	            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
185	            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
186	            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."
187	
188	      - name: Commit execution log to main
189	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
190	        env:
191	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
192	        run: |
193	          set -e
194	          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
195	          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
196	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
197	          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
198	          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
199	          cp execution.json "$DEST"
200	          git add "$DEST"
201	          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
202	          git push origin main
203	
204	      - name: Trigger agent improver
205	        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
206	        env:
207	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
208	          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
209	        run: |
210	          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
211	          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
212	            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
213	            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
214	            -f log_ref=main
215

file.content:
name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 60). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '60'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - name: Generate App installation token
        id: app-token
        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v1
        with:
          app-id: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ secrets.AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}

      - name: Resolve App bot identity for git commits
        id: app-user
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          APP_SLUG: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.app-slug }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          info=$(gh api "/users/${APP_SLUG}[bot]")
          echo "user-id=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .id)"       >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "user-login=$(echo "$info" | jq -r .login)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # App installation token; needs Contents + Pull requests + Workflows
          # write so the agent can push branches that add or modify
          # .github/workflows/*.
          token: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        env:
          BOT_LOGIN: ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-login }}
          BOT_ID:    ${{ steps.app-user.outputs.user-id }}
        run: |
          git config --global user.name  "${BOT_LOGIN}"
          git config --global user.email "${BOT_ID}+${BOT_LOGIN}@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --model claude-opus-4-8 \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the run log carefully:

1. **First command failed:** `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain` failed with error "Contains simple_expansion". This is because the workflow passes `TICKET_KEY` as an environment variable, but the agent tried to use it in a shell expansion (`"$TICKET_KEY"`). The Bash allowlist in the workflow has `Bash(jira issue view:*)` which should match, but the simple_expansion error suggests that the shell variable expansion is being blocked by the harness path validator.

2. **Retry succeeded:** The agent then ran `jira issue view "KAN-34" --plain` (using the literal value) which worked. This is the classic failed-then-retried pattern.
3. The remaining operations all completed successfully—reading, editing, staging, committing, pushing, and creating the pull request all went through without issues. The core problem is that the harness blocks simple variable expansion like `"$TICKET_KEY"` even though the prompt warns against compound commands but doesn't mention this restriction, and the workflow itself is passing the variable as an environment variable expecting it to be usable. The real issue is that the prompt instructs the agent to use a shell variable `$TICKET_KEY`, but the harness prevents variable expansion, so the agent needs to substitute the actual literal value from the context header instead. The simplest fix would be to reword the prompt instruction to explicitly tell the agent to use the literal ticket key value rather than the shell variable syntax.

content.text:
The failure is clear: the prompt tells the agent to run `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain` using shell variable expansion, but the harness blocks simple expansions ("Contains simple_expansion" error). The agent then retried with the literal key `KAN-34`. That's one failed call + one retry on the very first step of every run.

The prompt already tells the agent the ticket key is "prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`" in the context header — the agent can read the literal value from there. The fix is to remove the `$TICKET_KEY` shell variable from the example command and tell the agent to use the literal key.

input.old_string:
1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
input.new_string:
1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view <ticket-key> --plain` where
   `<ticket-key>` is the literal key from the context header above
   (e.g. `jira issue view KAN-34 --plain`). Shell variable expansion
   is blocked — do not write `"$TICKET_KEY"`. Note summary,
   description, acceptance criteria.

content.content: The file /home/runner/work/my-harness/my-harness/agents/junior-dev/prompt.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

tool_use_result.newString:
1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view <ticket-key> --plain` where
   `<ticket-key>` is the literal key from the context header above
   (e.g. `jira issue view KAN-34 --plain`). Shell variable expansion
   is blocked — do not write `"$TICKET_KEY"`. Note summary,
   description, acceptance criteria.
tool_use_result.originalFile:
You implement Jira tickets in this repository. The ticket key is
prepended above as `TICKET_KEY`. A feature branch is already checked
out at `origin/main`. Your job: implement the change, commit, push,
and open a Draft PR. Then stop.

## Environment

- `jira`, `gh`, `git`, `claude` on `PATH`. `JIRA_CONFIG_FILE` and
  `JIRA_API_TOKEN` set; `GH_TOKEN` set; git user configured.
- `jira issue view` is read-only — never call mutating jira commands.
- CWD is the repo on the dispatched feature branch. Don't switch
  branches; don't force-push.
- The Bash allowlist only permits the specific patterns listed in the
  workflow. Compound commands (pipes, `&&`, `;`-chained, sub-shells)
  are not allowed and will be rejected by the harness — run each step
  as its own Bash call. Don't try to bypass this.

## Workflow

1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
   summary, description, acceptance criteria.
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
   `Glob`, `Grep`. Verify paths exist before assuming.
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the
   ticket asks for. No drive-by refactoring.
4. Verify: if the change has obvious tests or a lint command, run
   them (`npm test`, `npm run lint`). Skip if there's nothing
   plausible to run.
5. Commit: `git add <paths>` then `git commit -m "<message>"`. Small
   focused commits or a single squash-style commit are both fine.
   Don't commit broken code.
6. Push and open PR (below).

## Finishing

Write the PR body to `.pr-body.md` with the `Write` tool first — passing
`--body "..."` with multi-line markdown (e.g. `## Summary`) is blocked by the
harness path validator. Then:

```sh
git push -u origin HEAD
gh pr create --draft \
  --base main \
  --title "[${TICKET_KEY}] <one-line summary>" \
  --body-file .pr-body.md
```

If you're stopping early because of the turn cap or a blocker, prefix
the title with `[WIP]` and explain in the body.

**PR body** (GitHub-flavored markdown) — include:

- **Summary** — 1–3 bullets on what changed and why.
- **Acceptance criteria** — tick the AC items the change addresses
  (`- [x] ...`); leave unaddressed ones unchecked with a note.
- **Jira** — `<JIRA_SERVER>/browse/<TICKET_KEY>`. Read `JIRA_SERVER`
  from `.jira/config.yml` with the `Read` tool — don't `cat` via
  Bash, shell expansion is blocked.
- **If `[WIP]`** — a `## Incomplete` section explaining what's missing.

After `gh pr create` returns, stop. Output no further text.

## Forbidden

- Editing the Jira ticket (description, status, comments).
- Modifying **existing** agents' prompts under `agents/*/` or
  **existing** workflow files under `.github/workflows/`. Adding a
  *new* agent directory or a *new* workflow file is allowed when the
  ticket asks for it.
- Switching branches, force-pushing, opening non-Draft PRs.
- Touching `.github/agent-logs/`.
- Working around `git push` or `gh pr create` failures via the GitHub
  Contents API, sub-agents, base64 file uploads, or any other
  out-of-band mechanism. If `git push` exits non-zero, treat it as a
  blocker (see below) — do not retry with workarounds.

## If `git push` or `gh pr create` fails

Stop trying to push the change. Open a `[WIP]` Draft PR documenting
the blocker:

1. If `git push` worked but `gh pr create` failed: the branch is on
   the remote already. Stop and let the workflow's fallback step
   open the PR.
2. If `git push` failed: capture the exact stderr in your final
   reply, then stop. The workflow's fallback step will not be able
   to recover either, but a clear stderr in the run log is what the
   reviewer needs.

Either way, **do not** invoke sub-agents, the GitHub Contents API,
or any other mechanism to force the change through. A clean failure
beats a half-successful workaround.

## If the ticket is wrong-scoped or impossible

Push whatever partial progress is correct (or no commits if you can't
take a single safe step) and open a `[WIP]` Draft PR with the blocker
explained. Stopping cleanly with a clear blocker is a successful run.

lines: -1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain`. Note
+1. Read the ticket: `jira issue view <ticket-key> --plain` where
+   `<ticket-key>` is the literal key from the context header above
+   (e.g. `jira issue view KAN-34 --plain`). Shell variable expansion
+   is blocked — do not write `"$TICKET_KEY"`. Note summary,
2. Plan: open the files/symbols the ticket references with `Read`,
3. Implement: edit with `Edit`/`Write`. Stay scoped to what the

input.command: git checkout -b agent-improver/junior-dev-fix-ticket-key-expansion && git add agents/junior-dev/prompt.md && git commit -m "Prompt fix: junior-dev — use literal ticket key, not \$TICKET_KEY shell expansion"
input.description: Create branch, stage change, and commit

content.content:
Switched to a new branch 'agent-improver/junior-dev-fix-ticket-key-expansion'
[agent-improver/junior-dev-fix-ticket-key-expansion fd39946] Prompt fix: junior-dev — use literal ticket key, not $TICKET_KEY shell expansion
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "Prompt fix: junior-dev — use literal ticket key, not \$TICKET_KEY shell expansion" --body "The first command in every run was `jira issue view \"\$TICKET_KEY\" --plain`, which the harness rejected with \"Contains simple_expansion\". The agent then retried with the literal key — a wasted failed-call + retry on step 1 of every run.

The prompt already prepends the literal key in the context header (e.g. \`TICKET_KEY: KAN-34\`). This edit updates the step-1 example to use the literal key and explicitly tells the agent not to write \`\"\$TICKET_KEY\"\`.

Evidence: \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/2026-06-18/27745830086.json\` — first Bash call fails with \"Contains simple_expansion\", second call with hardcoded key succeeds."

input.command: gh pr create --base main --title "Prompt fix: junior-dev — use literal ticket key, not shell expansion" --body "The first command in every run was jira issue view with TICKET_KEY shell variable, which the harness rejected with 'Contains simple_expansion'. The agent then retried with the literal key — a wasted failed-call plus retry on step 1 of every run. The prompt already prepends the literal key in the context header. This edit updates the step-1 example to use the literal key and explicitly tells the agent not to use shell variable expansion. Evidence: .github/agent-logs/junior-dev/2026-06-18/27745830086.json — first Bash call fails, second call with hardcoded key KAN-34 succeeds."

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/pantageepapa/my-harness/pull/77

The run had one concrete failure: the prompt told the agent to run `jira issue view "$TICKET_KEY" --plain` using a shell variable, but the harness blocks simple expansions. The agent failed, then retried with the hardcoded key `KAN-34`. The prompt edit removes the `$TICKET_KEY` example and tells the agent to substitute the literal key from the context header instead.

result:

The agent failed, then retried with the hardcoded key `KAN-34`. The prompt edit removes the `$TICKET_KEY` example and tells the agent to substitute the literal key from the context header instead.
